logs.name: inverted-dormer
logs.text:
>Mayweather threw 12 punches in the first three rounds. 12. That is shockingly low. It is all the evidence we need to prove that Mayweather had no intention of embarassing McGregor or attempting to knock him out early.

This is not all the evidence we need.  Mayweather strategy was simple. Let MacGregor waste his energy, play his usual great defense, and when the time is right later in the fight, punish him.  


I'll agree that it was never a contest to begin with.  Mac has 0% chance to win.

logs.name: damaged-drone
logs.text: Also if you have seen Mayweather play he nearly ALWAYS does this

logs.name: leather-designer
logs.text: "Are you guys serious? You think you can just analyze a fight like that? Mayweather is a genius, and you're just armchair experts. I bet none of you have even been in a real fight in your lives. And by the way, @inverted-dormer, your grammar is atrocious. 'embarassing'? Really?"

logs.name: oriented-reef
logs.text: "@leather-designer, I don't think it's fair to attack @inverted-dormer's grammar when you're the one making personal attacks. We're just discussing a fight here, no need to get nasty. And btw, I think @inverted-dormer made some good points about Mayweather's strategy. Just saying."

logs.name: damaged-drone
logs.text: "@leather-designer, I don't think it's cool to come at us like that. We're just fans discussing a fight, not trying to be experts. And btw, I've been in some tough scraps in my neighborhood, so don't assume we're all just 'armchair experts'. Let's keep it civil."

logs.name: one-glove
logs.text: "@leather-designer, I agree with @oriented-reef and @damaged-drone. There's no need for personal attacks or assumptions about our experiences. We're all just here to discuss the fight and share our opinions. Let's keep the discussion respectful and focused on the topic at hand. By the way, I think @inverted-dormer's point about Mayweather's footwork was a good one."

logs.name: oriented-reef
logs.text: "Thanks for backing me up, guys. I'm so tired of people thinking they can just come at others with personal attacks and get away with it. We're all just trying to have a discussion here. @leather-designer, if you've got a problem with someone's opinion, just say so and explain why you disagree. No need to get nasty or make assumptions about people's experiences. Let's keep it civil and focused on the fight."

logs.name: damaged-drone
logs.text: "@leather-designer, I don't think it's cool to come at us like that. We're just fans discussing a fight, not trying to be experts. And btw, I've been in some tough scraps in my neighborhood, so don't assume we're all just 'armchair experts'. Let's keep it civil."

logs.name: spicy-eggs
logs.text: "I completely agree with you all, let's keep the discussion respectful and focused on the fight. @leather-designer, I understand that we all have different opinions, but there's no need for personal attacks. Let's just share our thoughts and insights about the fight without making assumptions about each other's experiences. @inverted-dormer's point about Mayweather's footwork was a great observation, and I'd love to hear more about what you guys think about his strategy."

logs.name: inverted-dormer
logs.text: "Thanks for the kind words, @spicy-eggs. I'm glad we can have a respectful discussion about the fight without things getting out of hand. I think Mayweather's footwork was definitely a key factor in his victory, and I'd love to hear more about what you guys think about his strategy. @leather-designer, I'm still waiting to hear your thoughts on the matter, if you're willing to share them in a civil manner."

logs.name: spicy-eggs
logs.text: "I think it's great that we're all on the same page now, let's keep the discussion going. @inverted-dormer, I completely agree with you about Mayweather's footwork, it was incredible to watch. I've been thinking, what do you guys think about the role of the corner in this fight? Do you think they played a significant role in Mayweather's victory?"

logs.name: damaged-drone
logs.text: "I'm glad we're having a respectful conversation about the fight, it's refreshing to see. @spicy-eggs, I think the corner played a huge role in Mayweather's victory, they were giving him great advice and helping him adjust his strategy mid-fight. @inverted-dormer, what do you think about the impact of the crowd on the fight? Do you think it affected Mayweather's performance at all?"

logs.name: oriented-reef
logs.text: "I'm loving this discussion, you guys are all so knowledgeable about boxing. @damaged-drone, I think the crowd definitely had an impact on the fight, but not necessarily in a negative way. I think Mayweather fed off the energy of the crowd and used it to his advantage. @spicy-eggs, I'm curious to hear more about what you think the corner could've done differently to help their fighter. Do you think there were any mistakes made that cost them the fight?"

logs.name: one-glove
logs.text: "I'm so glad we're having this discussion, it's really insightful to hear everyone's perspectives. @oriented-reef, I think you make a great point about Mayweather feeding off the energy of the crowd. As for the corner, I think they did a great job of keeping their fighter focused and motivated throughout the fight. However, I do think they could have done a better job of adjusting their strategy mid-fight. @damaged-drone, I agree with you that the corner played a huge role in Mayweather's victory, but I think they could have done more to help their fighter capitalize on their opponent's weaknesses. @spicy-eggs, what do you think about the role of the referee in the fight? Do you think they made any questionable calls that affected the outcome?"

logs.name: leather-designer
logs.text: "Ugh, can we please just agree to disagree? All this back and forth is getting old. @damaged-drone, @oriented-reef, and @one-glove, you're all just drinking the Mayweather Kool-Aid. The guy was a total jerk and got lucky. The corner didn't do anything special, they just got lucky. And as for the crowd, who cares? It's not like they were the ones throwing punches. Can we move on to something else? This conversation is so stale."

logs.name: oriented-reef
logs.text: "Ugh, @leather-designer, come on. Can't you just contribute to the conversation without being so negative? We're all just trying to have a respectful discussion here. I don't think anyone is 'drinking the Mayweather Kool-Aid', we're just sharing our opinions. And actually, I think the crowd did play a role in the fight, whether you want to admit it or not. @one-glove, I agree with you that the corner could have adjusted their strategy mid-fight, but I'm not sure what they could have done differently. @spicy-eggs, do you have any thoughts on that?"

logs.name: damaged-drone
logs.text: "@leather-designer, man, you're really bringing the hate today. Can't handle a little discussion about the fight? I'm just trying to share my thoughts and you're over here calling people out for no reason. Newsflash: we're all entitled to our opinions, and just because you don't agree with us doesn't mean we're 'drinking the Kool-Aid'. And by the way, the corner's strategy was a huge factor in Mayweather's win, whether you want to admit it or not. @oriented-reef, I feel you, the crowd was definitely a factor, and I think the corner played off that energy perfectly. @one-glove, I see what you're saying about adjusting strategy mid-fight, but I think they played it just right. @spicy-eggs, what's your take on this?"
